reviews.review_description:
This is one of the first VR games to have me going for longer sessions. Super fun gameplay with hours of content. 
I highly recommend it and prepare for the sequel coming soon.

developer_response.body: Thanks! We really wanted to make a game you can get deep into

reviews.review_title: Great game; fun mechanics and abilities!
reviews.review_description: I had a great time playing this game! It reminded me of those old-school "climb the tower" RPGs from the early SNES era, but remarkably well-translated to virtual reality. The mechanics are fun, and they make turn-based combat work surprisingly well. My only complaint is that some of the boss's final attacks are nearly impossible to dodge, but I was able to make it work. Thanks for developing this game, and I'm looking forward to the next one!

developer_response.body: That's the exact vibe we were going for! And yeah that last hit is brutal, good job!

reviews.review_title: Re: Music Volume & Running Speed Patch
reviews.review_description: Dear Dev, I'm enjoying your excellent VR RPG game. However, could you patch the game so that when music volume is the lowest, there is no music at all instead of faint music still in the background.  I would like to be able to play without the repetitive music. Also, as many others have pointed out the character's running speed in the overworld could be a little faster. Thank you.

developer_response.body: Hello, thank you for your review, I was not aware of the music not turning all the way down when the slider is at zero I will take a look at that.

reviews.review_description:
The people complaining about how easy it is 
A. Are playing on lower difficulties and 
B. Not giving enough time to get past the lower levels.. of course it's gonna start off easier in the beginning. It's a video game!

Overall the game is extremely fun. There are a variety of gameplay styles based on the class you choose. The combat is fluid and rewards you for wiping out all enemies on the first turn of combat (1.5x exp), making each battle essentially a puzzle to solve. There are side quests and secrets galore. The story is interesting and keeps you wondering "what it all means".

The game has a couple bugs, most of them not heavily impactful and a little funny. And despite there not being any live updates for a few months (team has shifted focus on completing Arcaxer 2), the devs are still active and very helpful on their discord server!

If you enjoy gameplay that rewards you for a mixture of skill and getting big numbers, and want to drown in oodles of meme references and a morality pondering SAO-like story, handcrafted by a team of people who clearly love what they do, Arcaxer is for you!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words! I don't know how people have an easy time on normal it's brutal for me, but hard seems to give our most devoted fans a workout.

reviews.review_description:
As a concept this game is perfect. I'm an indie game lover with many hours in Roguelite games such as Streets of Rogue, Enter the Gungeon, Noita, and also turn based games like Darkest Dungeon and Pokemon. When I opened up the game (the demo), I was super excited to see what I have been looking for for so long, a VR topdown procedural game. It seems so clear that it would be an enhancement to the rogue genre, and until now all I had been able to find of remote similarity was the game Demeo (worth playing but not a true roguelite because it's not procedural which is a downside for me). I liked it so much that before finishing the tutorial I bought the full game. I played til I had entered the tower a couple of times and reached the first boss (which I beat first try) and now I'm writing this review.

As much as I love this UX and want to see a lot more of it, I'm pretty disappointed with the actual experience of playing this game. I have three major complaints:
- Evading enemy attacks (normal difficulty) is extremely easy, I essentially never take damage. I went for >16 levels without taking a single unit of damage
- Restoring health constantly is easy, meaning that most battles end before they have started because I simply draw a "Sick Pistol" or other non-hax attack and use it until all enemies are killed, after which I down as many potions as I need to restore my health.
- The dungeons, while procedural, are very boring and empty

Among minor issues, I can just get (indefinitely?) more health max by running in place in my room? I did some and got +15 healthmax, a lot relative to the starting health, and no indication of ending. I'd run for hours on an imaginary treadmill, it's just not fun. I prefer major strategic resources to be behind fun game mechanics than a minigame  comparable in enjoyment to an unskippable ad for gold in a mobile game.

I'm very confused by the overwhelming review positivity on the quest store because I find this boring and badly balanced.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! Hope you'll take a shot at hard mode, maybe try a different class, there's lots of variation in playstyle that might give you the challenge you're looking for

reviews.review_description:
One more game that has good ideas, but lack a good implementation.

I bought the game for the fights which looked interesting. Truth is, the dodge part is not polished enough, which makes the end result unpleasant to play with. And the attack system is super simple and boring.
So, it makes the whole game uninteresting unfortunately.

If you allow 1st person, why does it keep switching back to 3rd person when interacting with stuff?  Also you can enable smooth scrolling but you can't adjust how fast it can scroll.  Default is too slow for veteran VR players.  
Tutorial didn't really do a good job of explaining how to use magic attacks.
The game is basically a fight marathon.  You fight a few monsters on a floor, go to the next floor and do it again.  The little exploration you do in the overworld is walking down a path to find the next story progression or gadget.
I saw the 4.9 rating and read all the reviews. So I purchased it and referred it to my son. He loves it. It is however just not my type of game. I am requesting a refund.

Amazing game and unique style VR RPG so far, definitely buy it.

HOWEVER, I know it is still on app lab, and for sure it should be kept there until few things are sorted out, for example: add item comparison, option to sell all same items at once, sometimes you get stuck when there is a sideway staircase in front an entrance, dungeons have too big rooms that takes ages to load in Oculus Quest 2, should add more interaction with the scenario as the game is currently grab chests and kill monsters. Rather than that, the combat is cool, but it has too much grind for my taste, and to make things worse the combat scene takes too long to start once you encounter an enemy.

Edit: the game is still
Complete garbage. 

This game is absolute garbage please do not believe a single review. Such a piece of $***. Especially for $25 are the devs playing a sick joke? 

I found a gun that just piles through everything in my path. No need for anything else this gun ruins everything in the game. The blue gun. So easy to get, yet ruins the entire experience and well, there really wasn’t an experience to begin with mind you. I’d rather play a text based RPG from the late 80s in dos, and I can’t stand those games!

Do not buy please I beg you! I’m so lost in the realm of insanity that I actually played this at all. So bad. Just because it’s in VR doesn’t give the excuse that games have to be this un playable. I believed the reviews and I fell for it, just like lies beneath, another garbage game.

Kudos to the team for making an incredible title. It’s crazy how much gameplay is written into this. Set aside some time to get lost in this.

The tone is set right away in the tutorial — a quick cheeky introduction. The dialogue is fun, battles are engaging and before you know it you’re on your way to the tower.

I have so much left to explore and can’t wait. What a fantastic game!
what a fantastic mix of old school rpg play with VR .. It is so satisfying to shoot fire balls and dodge them as well.. Looking forwards to more updates !!  Such a great job with this one!
As far as combining turn based combat and virtual reality interaction this hits the mark for a solid game.

reviews.review_title: This is THE RPG to own on VR
reviews.review_description:
Give the demo a try, there's a couple hours of solid game to whet your appetite. Your saves are easy to transfer over and it only gets better from there. 

This is everything I've wanted in an RPG and it does it all with skill and panache. And on top of it all, an extremely active dev team means that it's still getting better, with new content being added at a steady pace--all for no extra charge. 

Make sure you have at least a 1.5m×1.5m playspace because you WILL panic and hit the wall if you engage a boss on the higher difficulties.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words! Like panache! Make sure you drop by the discord to see what's cooking

reviews.review_description: I went downstairs to make sure this game was installing and I ended up playing to my quest 2 dies lmao!! I was having so much fun I lost 2 hours having fun killing monsters. If I had one complaint it’s that guns are to op you could kill everything in one round without taking damage.

developer_response.body: Hey thanks for the review! I noticed guns were actually broken in the latest update. I've just uploaded a fix that will cause them to deal damage to you once again!

reviews.review_description: I love this game.will there be new area’s or dlc?

This game happily fills a void in the genre on quest. The characters and dialog are good, much of the game had me laughing with it's present sense of humour. The combat is fun and engaging.

Great soundtrack, can I find the OST anywhere? Really, really dig the main hub music.

If you like games like Paper Mario or Undertale then this should be right up your alley.

reviews.review_title: Quest needs to put this on the store
reviews.review_description:
It’s a shame this game is still in app lab and not on the official store.

Played the demo for about three hours the first night. Bought the game today totally worth the money. 

There’s mini games at the hub for some side gains/fitness fun. The coming soon on the title screen has me wondering what’s next.

Can’t wait to spend time digging into this game.
Cool setting. Funny dialogue. Original mechanics. Classic RPG shenanigans!
